entries.writeup:
I live under the bridge, so it’s dark every morning.
What would it be like if I woke up to the moon instead?
I think a lot about the light that reflects off the moon. Who is your sun, when the morning comes?
I think about the moon a lot, even when it’s so far and I can’t reach it. Why do I do that?

Waiting at Delancey, thinking about the dream that I had
I had a dream that a ‘wise’ old man
asked me to become religious. Telling me to go from atheism to scientism.
I told him no, religion can be problematic, though. It deems superiority. 
I think about my writing, and there are a lot of logical fallacies in my thinking.
I keep contradicting myself, the cost of being really self-aware and self-correcting, I guess. Hypocrisy. Will I ever stop evolving?
Even though I employ a reductionist thinking, physics can be really broken down to math.
But numbers confuse me.
entries.anxiety_notes: felt anxious at pride. started discussing future plans, and realised, I don't know what's next. lasted for a short-while.
entries.grief_notes: i am still in ways, isolating myself.

entries.writeup:
“It’s like everything is in my head, and I always knew, and I know, 
I’ve known ever since I was a kid.
but my thoughts are a mess, and there’s so much,
re-reading my own work and writing right now helps me connect the dots”
And to this, she responds that she should read her personal statement too.

“I was walking down Broadway to get coffee.
brain buzzing about the hex inverter, the sequence of logic gates. A pattern. A Brain and its logic gates. Nervous system.
Raviya barged into my room saying she barfed in McDonald’s. It’s funny, I think. The thought jars against the rest.
I was also thinking about space. Did I really leave anything behind on Earth to come here? What’s there on Earth to look back to? Free? Free! My friends are scattered in space too. How long will we all go on without the gravitational pull?
What do others think about, usually?
